context_prose: post-tool-use.sh is a single-line bash script wired to Claude Code's PostToolUse lifecycle event by hooks.json. After every tool invocation by the agent, Claude Code execs this script (with a 5s timeout) and consumes its stdout as a hookSpecificOutput JSON object. The script's only job is to emit a fixed JSON payload via `jq -n` whose `additionalContext` string nudges the agent toward calling `/learn` at completion boundaries and `/prepare` at start-of-work boundaries. The script takes no input from stdin, makes no decisions, and produces the same output on every invocation; freshness/relevance of the reminder is the agent's problem, not the script's. `set -euo pipefail` ensures any jq failure causes a non-zero exit (rather than silently emitting partial JSON), and the shebang pins bash so the pipefail/strictness semantics are portable.
name: post-tool-use

properties.name: Strict bash mode
properties.notes: Combined with the bash shebang on line 1 to guarantee pipefail semantics regardless of /bin/sh.
properties.statement: For all invocations of post-tool-use.sh, the script runs under `set -euo pipefail`, so any unset variable, command failure, or pipeline failure causes a non-zero exit rather than partial output.

properties.name: Single JSON object on stdout
properties.notes: Generated by `jq -n`, which guarantees well-formed JSON or a non-zero exit.
properties.statement: For all invocations, the script writes exactly one JSON object to stdout containing a single top-level key `hookSpecificOutput`.

properties.name: Hook event name is PostToolUse
properties.notes: Drift between this string and hooks.json's R4 registration would silently break Claude Code's hook routing.
properties.statement: For all invocations, `hookSpecificOutput.hookEventName` equals the literal string `PostToolUse`, matching the lifecycle event hooks.json registers this script under.

properties.name: additionalContext nudges /learn and /prepare
properties.notes: This is the reminder content; behavior is purely informational — Claude Code injects the string into the agent's context, the agent decides whether to act.
properties.statement: For all invocations, `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` is a non-empty string that mentions both `/learn` (for completion boundaries) and `/prepare` (for new-work boundaries).

properties.name: Deterministic, input-independent output
properties.notes: The script reads no input and branches on no state; this property is what makes the 5s timeout in hooks.json safely generous.
properties.statement: For all invocations, the stdout payload is byte-identical regardless of stdin, environment variables, working directory, or which tool just ran — the script is a pure constant emitter.

properties.name: Fits within hook timeout
properties.notes: Timeout is enforced by Claude Code, not the script; correlated with hooks.json R4 (timeout 5).
properties.statement: For all invocations on a healthy host, the script completes well within the 5-second timeout hooks.json declares for PostToolUse, since it forks a single `jq -n` and exits.

properties.name: No side effects
properties.notes: Contrast with session-start.sh which async-rebuilds the Go binary; post-tool-use is intentionally inert because it runs after every tool call.
properties.statement: For all invocations, the script performs no file writes, no network calls, no environment mutation, and starts no background processes — its only externally observable effect is the JSON it writes to stdout.
